body.title: [sprint] State inventory / leak audit — run every state kind through durability x readiness
body.description:
Workstream 2 of D-rewind-as-workflow-primitive. Enumerate every kind of state we hold and classify each on two axes — durability (ephemeral -> durable) and readiness (raw -> predigested). For each leak (ephemeral, or durable-but-raw), decide the fix: static write at a step boundary, or an active-maintenance minion. Known candidates: rejected design branches, advisor feedback, the live working set, 'what's next', build logs (-> digester, already ticketed). Gated on T-rewind-output-contracts existing first (the audit slots fixes into the contract table).

**Affected files:** TBD — the inventory lands as a doc; fixes spread across slate/memory/DB writes per the contract table.
**Design rules:** external-state-principle, docs-live-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — produce the inventory + per-leak remediation decision. OUT — executing the remediations (each becomes its own follow-up once classified).
**Test plan:** no tests because: this is an audit/inventory deliverable. Each remediation it spawns carries its own test plan.
**Completion criteria:** An inventory doc classifies every state kind on both axes; each ephemeral or durable-but-raw leak carries a remediation decision; the build-log digester and known leaks (rejected branches, advisor feedback, working set, what's-next) are accounted for.
body.intention: I intend that every kind of state we hold is classified on durability x readiness, with each leak assigned a concrete remediation (static write or active-maintenance minion).
